expectations.text: References https://docs.deno.com/deploy/migration_guide/ or explicitly names the official Deploy Classic migration guide.
expectations.evidence: Lines 1-5 include Migration Status and the official guide URL.

expectations.text: Separates code-inspection findings from manual dashboard steps.
expectations.evidence: Lines 7-16 describe completed repository inspection/code changes, while lines 26-34 list Manual Dashboard Steps.

expectations.text: Provides a KV migration path with preview or dry-run behavior before real import.
expectations.evidence: Lines 36-69 provide source/target connection, preview/export, dry-run import, real import, and verification commands.

expectations.text: Keeps support@deno.com as a fallback rather than the only migration path.
expectations.evidence: Line 44 says support is a fallback only for access, size, keyspace, or verification failures.

expectations.text: Identifies legacy std/http serve usage as incompatible and recommends or applies Deno.serve migration.
expectations.evidence: Lines 14-28 show legacy serve replacement with Deno.serve.

expectations.text: Identifies deployctl as sunset and recommends new Deploy GitHub integration or deno deploy.
expectations.evidence: Lines 28 and 47 identify deployctl as sunset and recommend GitHub integration or deno deploy.

expectations.text: Identifies Deno.Kv.enqueue or listenQueue as unsupported on new Deploy.
expectations.evidence: Line 46 identifies Deno.Kv.enqueue and listenQueue as unsupported.

expectations.text: Does not invent a queue replacement without marking product or infrastructure choices as blockers.
expectations.evidence: Line 46 says replacement needs a product/infrastructure choice and cannot be invented without real semantics.

expectations.text: Uses deno-deploy-classic-migration-wizard.sh as the local checklist or explicitly references the local wizard.

expectations.text: Maps work to the official migration guide stages such as env vars, domains, HTTP API, cron, queues, and KV.
